---
title: "NHTSA Troubleshoot"
author: "Patrick Torralba"
format: 
  html:
    theme: flatly
    fontsize: 0.8em
    code-fold: FALSE
    code-tools: TRUE
    code-copy: TRUE
    toc: TRUE
    toc-depth: 4
    smooth-scroll: TRUE
    embed-resources: TRUE
    fig-align: center
editor: visual
---

# Library
```{r}
#| label: library
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| echo: true

library(tidyverse)

```

# Import Raw Data

## Issue

* The issue here is that R is saying certain rows are NA when they actually have data
  * For example, ST_CASE == 131083 is supposed to have 1 row. But the df_v2 dataset has multiple duplicates.
    * Therefore, there must be something wrong in the merging process 

```{r}
#| label: vehicle-2018-v1
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| echo: true  

vehicle2018_v1 <- read_csv("Raw Data/2018/FARS2018NationalCSV/vehicle.CSV") %>% 
  select(ST_CASE, SPEC_USE)

view(vehicle2018_v1)
```
